summary: There is broad professional consensus among breast surgeons to attain negative margins in breast-conservation surgeries, driven by established surgical classifications and standard protocols aimed at minimizing local recurrences. This consensus stems from widespread recognition that precise excision—such as in lumpectomies or quadrantectomies—is essential for effective cancer control and long-term patient outcomes.
